archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Top-level CI pipeline entry script that orchestrates analysis, PRD validation, zone health checks, and architecture policy enforcement.
Orchestrates Claude Code integration setup: auto-approves MCP tools, writes workflow skills, and registers MCP servers via CLI.
Help system with Levenshtein typo correction, keyword search with relevance scoring, and ANSI-formatted command registry navigation.
Adaptive workflow engine that analyzes run history metrics and produces auto-applicable config recommendations.
Review gate service that collects git diffs, prompts user for approval, and reverts changes on rejection before task finalization.
Spin detection utility tracking consecutive empty-turn counts to abort runaway text-generation loops.
Stuck-task detector counting consecutive hard-failure runs to exclude tasks from auto-selection.
Extracts structured RunSummary (files changed/read, commands, tests) from ToolCallRecord arrays via pattern-based inference.
Workflow analysis engine scanning run history to detect bottlenecks and surface optimization suggestions.
Backward-compatibility shim re-exporting validateCompletion from the canonical validation module.
CLI provider agent loop: spawns claude/codex subprocess, parses streaming JSON events, handles retries, enforces token budgets and spin detection.
Background heartbeat that periodically writes lastActivityAt and peak RSS to prevent dashboard staleness during long tool calls.
API provider agent loop orchestrating turn-based Claude API calls with message pruning, tool dispatch, token budget checks, and completion validation.
Shared lifecycle helpers (brief prep, dry run, status transitions, run init, review gating, post-task testing) reused by both API and CLI loops.
Formats and prints task metadata (title, ID, priority, parent chain, criteria count) before agent execution begins.
Checks whether accumulated input+output tokens exceed the configured budget; returns exceeded flag and remaining allowance.
Token usage utilities: Codex usage mapping, aggregate accumulation, and formatted display strings, re-exported from llm-client foundation.
Assembles markdown task briefs by locating actionable tasks, collecting inherited requirements, and formatting for agent consumption.
Builds system prompts with agent rules, project context, workflow steps, and provider-specific tool notes.
Backward-compatibility shim re-exporting TOOL_DEFINITIONS and dispatchTool from the canonical tools/dispatch module.
CLI help command providing command summaries, usage patterns, flags, and examples using formatHelp() for consistent presentation.
Command validation utility with three layers: block shell operators, allowlist executable checking, and dangerous pattern detection.
Type definitions for guard configuration: PolicyLimitsConfig (rate limits, resource budgets) and GuardConfig (blocked paths, allowed commands).
Path validation utility rejecting null bytes, preventing directory escape, and blocking glob-pattern paths without external dependencies.
Session-aware policy engine tracking cumulative resource usage with sliding-window rate limiting and audit trail enforcement.
Single-point re-export gateway concentrating all runtime imports from the rex package (store, tree, task selection, types).
Collects time-series snapshots of concurrent process counts, memory utilization, and per-task resource metrics with ring-buffer history.
Backward-compatibility shim re-exporting execShell from the canonical tools/exec-shell module.
Backward-compatibility shim re-exporting process execution functions (exec, execStdout, spawnTool, ProcessPool) from llm-client foundation.
Process lifecycle validation service: orphan detection, dead-PID lock file cleanup, stale-run recovery, and resource threshold monitoring with audit trail.
ProcessLimiter class manages PID-based lock files for cross-process concurrency control — stateful service with lifecycle methods
SystemMemoryMonitor class reads platform-specific memory APIs and implements a monitoring interface — stateful service
MemoryThrottle class encapsulates throttling/rejection decisions based on live memory readings — stateful service
RuntimePool class manages a pool of reusable Node.js worker processes with lifecycle management
ProcessMemoryTracker class tracks RSS samples over time and detects leaks — stateful monitoring service
ExecutionQueue class implements a bounded priority FIFO queue with concurrency control — stateful service
Pure formatting helpers (formatQueueStatus, formatQueueStatusJson) with no state — utility functions
Pure priority resolution functions (resolveSchedulingPriority, extractPriorityFromTags) — stateless utility helpers
Single pure function simpleGlobMatch for pattern matching — minimal shared utility
Interface and type definitions (ToolGuard, ToolContext, RexToolHandlers) — boundary contract types with no logic
dispatchTool routes tool calls to implementations and owns TOOL_DEFINITIONS — central dispatch service layer
execShell is a shared wrapper around exec used by multiple tool implementations — infrastructure utility
File operation tool implementations (read, write, list, search) — stateless but domain-rich service functions
toolGit implements an allowlisted git subcommand tool — domain service function
Rex PRD mutation tools (update status, append log, add subtask) with completion and validation logic — service layer
toolRunCommand implements guarded shell execution — domain service function
runPostTaskTests orchestrates test discovery, runner detection, and scoped execution — service with non-trivial business logic
validateCompletion checks git diff and optionally runs tests before marking tasks done — validation service
createApiClient builds an Anthropic SDK-backed provider with retry logic — API client service
Pure detection and validation functions (detectCliAvailability, validateApiKey, diagnoseAuth) — stateless utility helpers
createCliClient spawns the claude binary and parses its output — process-spawning provider service
createCodexCliClient spawns codex exec with temp file output capture — process-spawning provider service
createClient factory with auto-detection of API vs CLI provider — client factory / service entry point
Low-level process execution abstractions (exec, spawnTool, spawnManaged) used across the package — shared infrastructure utility
Pure ANSI formatting helpers (formatHelp, bold, cyan, etc.) — stateless utility functions
Single pure function toCanonicalJSON for deterministic serialization — minimal utility
createLLMClient vendor-neutral factory supporting Claude and Codex with fallback detection — top-level service factory
loadLLMConfig reads .n-dx.json with legacy fallback — configuration loader
Vendor-neutral type definitions (LLMConfig, LLMVendor, LLMClient) with no logic — pure types file
deepMerge and loadProjectOverrides for merging .n-dx.json overrides — config utility helpers
Pure constants file exporting PROJECT_DIRS object — no logic, just named string constants for directory names.
Defines the LLMProvider interface, ProviderInfo, ProviderAuthMode, and capability types — pure type definitions with no runtime logic.
Implements the ProviderRegistry class with dynamic registration and config-driven provider selection — orchestration logic, not a passive store.
Manages active provider lifecycle, vendor switching, and session state — stateful service layer wrapping the registry.
Pure Levenshtein-based typo correction helpers with no side effects — shared utility consumed by multiple CLI packages.
Stateless parsing helpers for token usage from API and CLI formats — pure utility with no state or side effects.
Manages acknowledged findings state: hashing, reading/writing the acknowledged store to disk — stateful I/O service.
Shared constants and pure utilities (model defaults, token usage helpers) extracted to break circular deps — no state or I/O.
Post-processing pipeline step that detects over-granular LLM output and triggers secondary re-consolidation prompts.
Decomposes high-level proposals into more granular items via LLM calls — business logic orchestration.
Pure bigram similarity scoring and deduplication logic for scan results — no I/O or state.
Computes structural diffs between existing PRD tree and new proposals — pure transformation utility.
Extracts PRD items from LLM responses and source files — parsing and orchestration logic with I/O.
Pure file extension/path validation helpers shared across the analyze module.
Implements guided/interactive analysis flow — orchestrates LLM calls and user input for analyze pipeline.
Singleton LLM client and spawnClaude() function — manages provider config and routes prompts to the active LLM.
Generates LLM-powered reasoning for item modifications during the analyze pipeline.
Constructs structured Proposal objects from scan results, deduplicating and prioritizing before LLM submission.
Core reasoning engine: sends proposals to LLM and parses structured PRD item suggestions back.
Reconciles LLM-proposed changes with the existing PRD tree — merges, updates, and conflict resolution logic.
Reshapes LLM reasoning output into canonical proposal format — transformation step in the analyze pipeline.
File-system scanners that read test files, docs, and sourcevision output to produce ScanResult items — I/O-heavy service.
Pure validation logic for proposed PRD modifications — checks constraints without side effects.
Static help definition map and formatter — pure constants and display data, no command logic.
MCP tool handler implementations — each function encapsulates one tool's business logic against the PRD store.
Registers MCP tools with the McpServer and sets up stdio transport — the MCP subcommand entry point.
Shared CLI input validation helpers (level, status, etc.) — pure validation functions with no state.
Detects duplicate and conflicting recommendations against the existing PRD tree using bigram similarity.
Atomic PRD item creation from accepted recommendations with validation pipeline — business logic orchestration.
Single exported string constant defining the default workflow prompt — pure data, no logic.
Defines BUILTIN_ARCHETYPES catalog — structural role patterns as constants, no logic.
Pure heuristic classification functions (isBreakingChange, inferSignificance) with no side effects.
Orchestrates branch work collection by diffing PRD completion state vs. base branch — business logic with I/O.
Filters PRD items by branch lifecycle — stateful business logic reading git history.
Persistent read/write storage for branch work records with Zod validation — data layer for branch work state.
Generates deterministic architectural findings (god functions, dead code, tight coupling) from call graph metrics.
Parses function definitions and call expressions, resolves cross-file calls, and computes call graph metrics.
Matches files against archetype signals and calls Claude for unclassified files — classification business logic.
Vendor-neutral LLM client wrapper around @n-dx/llm-client with module-level config state management.
Reads .rex/prd.json and computes completion stats, timelines, and timestamp inconsistencies.
Identifies React components, JSX usage, route modules, and server-side API routes from source files.
Generates CONTEXT.md — dense XML-tagged codebase summary for AI context windows.
Batched LLM zone enrichment orchestrator with retry logic and severity reassessment passes.
Defines pass configuration and constructs prompts for enrichment passes — configuration data for the enrich pipeline.
Pure JSON parsing, finding extraction, and deduplication utilities for LLM enrichment responses.
Per-zone incremental enrichment mode — orchestrates LLM calls with incremental state updates.
Main enrichment pipeline orchestrator coordinating LLM-assisted zone analysis across multiple passes.
Uses TypeScript compiler API to extract imports, resolve dependencies, and detect circular chains.
Walks directory tree, classifies files by role and language, computes line counts — core inventory analysis.
Pure text-generation utility that builds llms.txt output from analysis data — no side effects, no CLI/route handling.
Deterministic Louvain modularity optimization algorithm — pure graph algorithm with no side effects.
Manages analysis metadata (git info, timestamps, module status) with disk I/O read/write operations.
Groups findings, assigns priorities, and derives actionable next steps from analysis results.
Computes zone risk metrics (cohesion/coupling) and emits deterministic findings for governance thresholds.
Detects HTTP routes from JSDoc comments and Express/Hono/Koa method call patterns.
Tracks and formats LLM token consumption across enrichment phases — pure accumulation and formatting utilities.
Merges pre-analyzed monorepo members into unified .sourcevision/ output — aggregation orchestration with I/O.
Resolves external npm imports to sibling workspace members and computes cross-repo zone dependencies.
Detects sub-analyses, promotes zones with prefixed IDs, and builds workspace reference structures.
Pure hash computation over zone content for cache invalidation — extracted to break circular dependency.
Emits per-zone output files (context.md, summary.json) — a pure data transformation/emission service
Runs Louvain community detection on the import graph to produce architectural zone data
Provides formatted help text definitions for CLI commands — no command logic, pure data/formatting
Implements the `mcp` CLI subcommand, starting the MCP server over stdio or HTTP
Implements the `serve` CLI subcommand, delegating to the web package via spawn
Generates a structured PDF report from analysis data — a document generation service
Pure functions transforming BranchWorkRecord into PR markdown — no I/O, no side effects
Merge utilities in the util/ directory
Canonical JSON sorting helpers used across output generation
Filesystem-invalidated cache layer for computed aggregation results
Collects and aggregates time-series metrics for concurrent hench process execution
Explicitly a gateway module — re-exports sourcevision imports for the web server
Incremental aggregator maintaining in-memory per-task token usage with change detection
TCP port availability detection and dynamic allocation utilities
Contains only type/enum definitions for PR markdown refresh diagnostic codes — no runtime logic.
Centralized sync file I/O helpers for reading/writing prd.json — infrastructure utility with high fan-in.
Stateful per-process memory tracking with ring buffers and threshold warnings — business logic service.
Wires up the usage cleanup scheduler lifecycle — a single-purpose setup/registration helper.
Explicitly documented as a re-export gateway concentrating all web→rex cross-package imports.
Express-style HTTP route handlers for analysis/proposal endpoints (analyze, smart-add, batch-import).
HTTP route handlers in routes-rex directory — handles execution-related endpoints.
HTTP route handler for health-check endpoints.
HTTP route handlers for PRD item CRUD operations.
HTTP route handler for PRD prune operations.
HTTP route handlers for read-only PRD data endpoints.
HTTP route handlers for requirements-related endpoints.
Shared helpers and thin wrappers over rex functions used across route modules — high fan-in utility.
Stateful in-memory inverted index with lazy build, mtime-based invalidation, and scoring logic — a self-contained service.
Explicitly documented as types-only module with no runtime code — cross-zone type definitions.
Business logic for tracking and querying task usage data
Scheduled cleanup service that runs periodic maintenance tasks
WebSocket server setup and connection management
Constants and type definitions for data file paths/names
Shared utility for culling graph nodes based on criteria
Shared utility for deduplicating in-flight requests
Viewer entry point that bootstraps the client-side application
Data loading and fetching orchestration for the viewer
Fetch pipeline orchestrating HTTP request flow
Utility for coalescing multiple messages into batches
WebSocket client pipeline managing connection and message flow
Service that monitors for and responds to crash conditions
Utility that gates DOM updates based on performance thresholds
Service coordinating graceful degradation strategies under load
Service monitoring memory usage and triggering alerts
Utility gating response processing based on buffer state
Utility for batching updates to reduce render thrashing
Service orchestrating polling lifecycle and coordination
Utility handling polling restart logic and backoff
State container tracking polling status and configuration
Utility detecting tab visibility changes to pause/resume polling
Utility managing tick timing intervals for polling
Standalone infrastructure module that bridges tab visibility API to tick timer lifecycle — no framework dependencies, pure logic with factory pattern.
Schema version migration utility — applies data migrations for backward compatibility, no UI or framework concerns.
Shared viewer utility module with path helpers and backward-compatible re-exports from the visualization layer.
Pure helper module providing zone color palette and lookup functions for visualization components.
Flow-diagram data builders that transform zone/call/import data into edge and node arrays — pure transformation logic, no UI.
Single metric classification helper (meterClass) for visualization display — focused utility with no state or framework deps.
Orchestration module that runs a multi-step PR validation pipeline (build + rex validate), exposing runPRCheck as a callable service with CLI entry point.
Service module that refreshes and writes sourcevision dashboard artifact metadata — stateless operation with clear business logic.
Builds and validates a structured refresh execution plan from CLI flags — pure planning logic exported as a service function.
Provides snapshot, validation, and rollback operations for refresh state — service-layer logic managing file system state transitions.
Server orchestration service module exposing runWeb — manages foreground/background server lifecycle, PID files, port resolution, and subcommands (stop/status).
